channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Federal judge allows Trump's mass firings of federal workers to move forward
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge in Washington has allowed President Donald Trump’s mass firings of federal workers to move forward. U.S. District Judge Christopher Cooper decided Thursday he could not grant a motion from unions representing the workers to temporarily block the layoffs.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Nearly 200 Venezuelan migrants are flown home from Guantanamo Bay, with a layover in Honduras
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Nearly 200 Venezuelan immigrants to the U.S. were returned to their home country after being detained at Guantanamo Bay, in a flurry of flights that forged an unprecedented pathway for U.S. deportations. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rep. Burchett: Congress to blame if Trump agenda fails
item.description: GOP Tennessee Rep. Tim Burchett tells NewsNation he knows whose fault it will be if Donald Trump's agenda doesn't succeed.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson discourages DOGE stimulus checks idea
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) threw cold water on the idea of using savings from the Trump administration’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) government-slashing activities to send checks to Americans.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate confirms Kash Patel to lead FBI
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Kash Patel to serve as the head of the FBI, approving a pick with a string of controversial statements who has been accused by Democrats of directing a purge of bureau employees. Patel was confirmed in a 51-49 vote. Two Republicans, Sens.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: The science behind snow needles
item.description: When snow falls, the temperature and humidity conditions in the dendritic growth zone, often referred to as the "snow growth zone," help shape these ice crystals.
item.dc:creator: Tommy House, NewsNation

item.title: IRS lays off 6,700 employees, torpedoing Democratic enforcement boost
item.description: The IRS fired 6,700 employees Thursday, a government official told NewsNation, the sister television network of The Hill. The employees were designated as probationary, meaning they were working for the agency on a trial basis prior to becoming full staff members.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, WGN 9

item.title: Trump may buy used planes to serve as Air Force One amid Boeing delays
item.description: It's not the planes, rather the heavy modification to make them suitable for the requirements of presidential travel and the top-level security clearances required for those involved, that has added to the cost and delays.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Masked robbers point ARs at guard in bold ATM heist caught on chilling video
item.description: A group of brazen suspects were seen pointing assault rifles in the face of a cash-in-transit guard before making off with large sums of cash from an ATM machine, wild video released by police in Tennessee shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli UFC fighter talks antisemitism at self-defense seminar as Hamas hostages mark 500 days
item.description: Israeli UFC fighter Natan Levy marks 500th day of Hamas holding hostages with seminar aimed at teaching Jews how to defend themselves from possible antisemitic attacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: McConnell won't seek reelection to Senate
item.description: Sen. Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) announced Thursday that he is not seeking reelection in 2026, bringing an end to the longest-serving Senate leader’s political career.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Menendez brothers discuss 'bullying and trauma' in prison in rare public remarks, new podcast interview
item.description: Convicted parental killer Erik Menendez discusses the "trauma and bullying" of his life in a California prison in a new interview from behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Most worried DOGE cuts could hurt services in their communities: Survey
item.description: Most U.S. adults in the latest survey, 62 percent, disagreed with the notion that the president is authorized to terminate federal employees who do not align with his views or agenda.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Trump Education pick Linda McMahon clears committee, heads to full Senate
item.description: Linda McMahon, President Trump’s pick for Education secretary, passed the Senate Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions (HELP) Committee on Thursday morning, sending her confirmation to the full Senate.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Unions sue over Trump admin efforts to fire probationary federal employees
item.description: A coalition of government employee unions sued late Wednesday over the Trump administration’s efforts to fire employees still in their probationary period across the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Trump approval rating appears to be underwater one month into second term: Survey
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating appears to be underwater as many suggest he may be overstepping his authority, according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Arkansas victims of mortuary theft scheme inspire push for law banning sale of human remains
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — A year had passed since Doneysha Smith suffered a stillbirth and the memories of her loss lingered in 2023.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: IRS plans to lay off 7,000 probationary workers beginning Thursday, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The IRS will lay off roughly 7,000 workers in Washington and around the country beginning Thursday, a person familiar with the plans told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Viral video shows Indiana police punch man during arrest. Here's what we know
item.description: William Jerome Armstrong was arrested Tuesday afternoon in the parking lot of a Walmart after police say they saw his car parked in an emergency fire lane.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, Fox 5

item.title: Aircrafts collide in midair near Arizona airport; 2 reported dead
item.description: The NTSB said one of the airplanes landed "uneventfully" after the collision, while the other crashed.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Tens of millions of dead people aren't getting Social Security checks, despite Trump and Musk claims
item.description: The Trump administration is falsely claiming that tens of millions of dead people over 100 years old are receiving Social Security payments.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A comprehensive look at DOGE's firings and layoffs so far
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Thousands of federal government employees have been shown the door in the first month of President Donald Trump's administration as the White House and its Department of Government Efficiency fire both new and career workers, tell agency leaders to plan for “large-scale reductions
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USDA working to rescind terminations for fired officials working on bird flu efforts
item.description: The accidental firings come amid the Trump administration’s sweeping push to reduce the size of the government and cut the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Illinois governor to back 'screen-free schools' and join national trend to ban cellphones in class
item.description: Illinois Gov. JB Pritzker is aiming to improve student achievement, social interaction and the mental health of public school students by proposing a statewide ban on cellphones in classrooms, an idea that is rapidly gaining traction nationally regardless of political persuasion.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Father-son duo survives night in Utah wilderness after finding 'miracle' backpack
item.description: In January, a 15-year-old boy was rescued from a southern Utah trail, but left his backpack behind. Weeks later, a father and son found that backpack and used it to survive.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, WGN 9

item.title: Leader of cultlike 'Zizian' group linked to 6 killings in multiple states ordered held without bail
item.description: The Zizians have been tied to the killing of U.S. Border Patrol Agent David Maland near the Canadian border in January and five other homicides in Vermont, Pennsylvania and California.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, Fox 5

item.title: Trump will sign new executive orders while his first joint TV interview with Musk airs in prime time
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday was set to sign new executive orders from his Florida home while his first joint TV interview with adviser Elon Musk airs in prime time.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge refuses to block DOGE’s access to student borrower data
item.description: A judge is also set to rule soon on whether to block DOGE from accessing systems at seven federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Delta jet flips upside down on a snowy Toronto runway and all 80 aboard survive
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — A Delta Air Lines jet flipped on its roof while landing Monday at Toronto’s Pearson Airport, but all 80 people on board survived and those hurt had relatively minor injuries, the airport’s chief executive said.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Congress closing in on shutdown deadline with no clear plan
item.description: Congress is struggling to strike a deal to keep the government funded as a looming deadline to prevent a shutdown next month gets closer. Lawmakers are less than a month away from a mid-March date to pass legislation to prevent a funding lapse — or risk the first shutdown in years.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Schools around the US confront anxiety over Trump's actions on immigration
item.description: In Fresno, California, social media rumors about impending immigration raids at the city's schools left some parents panicking — even though the raids were all hoaxes.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Utahns participate in nationwide Presidents' Day protests against Trump, Musk
item.description: Hundreds of Utahns gathered at the State Capitol on Monday, Feb. 17, to participate in an hours-long protest against President Donald Trump — but not everyone at the Capitol shared the same viewpoint.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan May, ABC 4

item.title: A look at recent aviation disasters in North America
item.description: The crash of a Delta Air Lines plane in Toronto is the fourth major aviation disaster in North America in the last month. Here's a look at each of the crashes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump begins firings of FAA staff just weeks after fatal DC plane crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has begun firing several hundred Federal Aviation Administration employees, upending staff on a busy air travel weekend and just weeks after a January fatal midair collision at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hundreds of FAA workers laid off amid staffing shortages, raising safety concerns
item.description: Hundreds of probationary workers with the Federal Aviation Administration were laid off over the weekend, raising concerns about air traffic safety as the agency struggles to meet hiring goals amid increased air traffic.
item.dc:creator: Ben Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: SpaceX employees visiting FAA amid reported firings
item.description: Members of the SpaceX team will "envision how we can make a new, better, modern and safer system," Sean Duffy said.
item.dc:creator: Steven Joachim, WGN 9

item.title: Here are the agencies purging federal workers
item.description: The moves come as President Trump's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), led by tech billionaire Elon Musk, have exacerbated their efforts to cut down on government spending — in some cases looking to dismantle whole agencies or departments.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9
